events.name: SpX-34 Launch
events.description: SpaceX is targeting Friday, May 15, for Falcon 9’s launch of Dragon’s 34th Commercial Resupply Services (CRS-34) mission for NASA to the International Space Station from Space Launch Complex 40 (SLC-40) at Cape Canaveral Space Force Station in Florida. Liftoff is targeted for 6:05 p.m. ET.

events.name: SpX-34 Dock
events.description: After an approximate 36-hour flight, Dragon will autonomously dock with the orbiting laboratory on Sunday, May 17, at around 7:00 a.m. ET.

events.name: 95P Reboost 1
events.description: A planned orbital maneuver using the engines of the Progress 95 spacecraft to raise the International Space Station’s altitude and maintain its operational orbit.

events.name: Payload Installation
events.description: ISS crew members installed the TIGERS-X experiment module into the ICE Cubes Facility.

events.name: Calibration
events.description: System Calibration with Space Application Services

events.name: Task Day 1
events.description: The first day of TIGERS-X experiment operations began with real experiment runs on Channel 1 and Channel 3. Teams monitored payload performance, fluid behavior, telemetry, and command responses during the operational sequence.

events.name: Task Day 2
events.description: The second day of TIGERS-X experiment operations began with real experiment runs on Channel 2 and Channel 4. Teams monitored payload performance, fluid behavior, telemetry, and command responses during the operational sequence.
